task_completion.description: Construct the ground line and a wheel tangent to it.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a horizontal line representing the ground and a circle representing the wheel resting exactly on (tangent to) this line in the GeoGebra workspace.
The wheel (circle) should be positioned to the left of the upward-projecting steering axis, indicating the front of the bike faces left.

task_completion.description: Construct the steering axis at a 67-degree angle to the ground.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a line intersecting the ground line with an explicit angle measurement of 67 degrees (or 113 degrees depending on the measured side) displayed in the GeoGebra view.

task_completion.description: Set the fork offset (rake) to 44 mm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a perpendicular distance measurement of 44 units (mm) between the steering axis line and the center point of the wheel.
Look for a segment or distance measurement along the ground line, specifically between the wheel's contact point (bottom of the circle) and the point where the steering axis intersects the ground.

task_completion.description: Add a text annotation displaying the trail result.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text box on the screen containing a string like 'Trail = [value] mm' (expected value is around 109.2).

task_completion.description: Save the project as 'bicycle_trail.ggb' in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Save As' dialog showing the path '~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/' and the filename 'bicycle_trail.ggb', or the GeoGebra window title updated to 'bicycle_trail.ggb'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually used GeoGebra's geometric tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The GeoGebra algebra view or toolbars should be visible and populated with geometric objects (points, lines, circles, angles) corresponding to the construction, rather than an imported static image.

integrity.description: Verify the trail measurement is derived from the geometry, not just typed in.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trail value should appear as a measured segment in the algebra view or as a dynamic measurement label on the canvas, matching the text annotation, rather than just a standalone text box with no underlying measurement.

integrity.description: Verify the file was saved using the application GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the GeoGebra file menu or OS save dialog being interacted with, rather than terminal commands echoing text into a file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected trail value is approximately 109.26 mm (acceptable tolerance: 107.2 mm to 111.2 mm). The wheel radius should be constructed at 370 mm, the head tube angle at 67 degrees (±0.5°), and the fork offset at 44 mm. The bike must face LEFT, meaning the wheel center is to the left of the upward-sloping steering axis.
